description: Harder evals with complex fake codebase (protos, Zustand, URL-driven state, cross-module coordination)

key_findings: With-skill specs have ZERO implementation leaks across all 3 evals (0 failures on no_implementation_leaks, no_code_fences, no_ts_interfaces, no_json_shapes, no_proto_definitions)
Without-skill specs leaked implementation details in 2/3 evals: hook names + file paths (annotations), exact line numbers (alert-templates)
Both versions produced zero code fences — the harder codebase did not induce code blocks unlike the real specs
The implementer_can_start failure on dashboard-chat is shared by both versions — it's a genuine spec gap (unresolved save policy), not a skill issue
With-skill specs use behavioral scope items (100% compliance); without-skill specs also score well here (matching template structure)

comparison_with_previous_iterations.iteration_4_original: 61% pass rate on easy evals (toy codebase, 11 assertions)
comparison_with_previous_iterations.iteration_5_improved: 97% pass rate on HARD evals (complex codebase, 11 assertions per eval)
comparison_with_previous_iterations.iteration_5_baseline: 88% pass rate on hard evals (no skill)
